# Copilot code review instructions

This repository holds userstyles (`*.user.css`, Stylus) and userscripts
(`*.user.js`, Tampermonkey), organized as `css/<domain>/` and `js/<domain>/`.
Focus reviews on the points below.

## Metadata header (highest priority)

Each custom file starts with a metadata header that `npm run lint`
(`packages/css-meta.js`, `packages/js-meta.js`) validates. Flag a changed file
when:

- A required key is missing. JS requires `downloadURL`, `match`, `grant`, and
  `icon` in addition to the CSS keys.
- `namespace` is not `tomacheese.com`, or `author` is not
  `Tomachi <tomachi@tomacheese.com> (https://github.com/book000)`.
- `homepageURL` / `supportURL` differ from the fixed
  `https://github.com/book000/browser-user-customs[/issues]` values.
- `updateURL` (and `downloadURL` for JS) does not match the file's actual path
  under `master/css/` or `master/js/` — a copied file with a stale path is a
  common mistake.
- An existing file was modified but its `version` was not bumped. The CI version
  check rejects a changed file that keeps the same version.

## Placement and scope

- A new custom lives under the directory matching its target domain; flag files
  placed in the wrong domain directory or at a wrong depth.
- Userscript `@match` patterns should be scoped to the intended pages, not
  overly broad (e.g. avoid `*://*/*` unless clearly intended).

## What NOT to flag

- Hard-coded selectors, site-specific CSS hacks, and `!important` are inherent to
  userstyles targeting third-party sites — do not report them as code smells.
- Each custom file is standalone; do not suggest extracting shared modules or a
  build step across files.
- `dist/` is generated by `generate-pages` and is not committed; do not review it.
